para_1: You grew up hearing there's never enough. Money was always tight. Your parents stressed about every expense. Even when you get a little extra now, you feel like it will disappear. You hold onto things you don't need, just in case. You buy the cheapest option, even if it breaks faster. Scarcity mindset makes you believe that money is always about to run out. So you play it safe, avoid risks, and miss opportunities. You might turn down a better job because you're afraid of losing what you have. You don't invest because you can't afford to lose anything. This mindset was installed when you were young, watching adults stress about money. Your brain learned that money equals fear. Research shows that scarcity actually changes how your brain works. When you're constantly worried about not having enough, your brain focuses on immediate problems and can't think long-term. Breaking this means recognizing the fear and asking, is this based on current reality or old programming? Most of the time, it's old programming.
